meta.document: Wisconsin DJI Fleet Grounding — Market Intelligence Brief

headline_facts.source: Oregon/NASAO White Paper Table 2 — direct state-reported data
headline_facts.context: Wisconsin is listed in the 'Severe (Majority or full fleet grounded)' category alongside Oregon, Georgia, Indiana, New York, and Colorado. 100% fleet impact is the worst-case classification in the survey.
headline_facts.note_on_numbers: The white paper reports 1 airframe for Wisconsin DOT/aviation specifically. This is state transportation/aviation agency data only — does NOT include local law enforcement, county agencies, municipal programs, or commercial operators. The actual Wisconsin-wide exposure is substantially larger.

trigger_1_omb_m2602.name: OMB Memorandum M-26-02
trigger_1_omb_m2602.effect: Implemented ASDA provisions effective December 22 2025. Prohibits use of federal funds to procure, operate, or maintain drones from covered Chinese manufacturers (DJI, Autel). Applies to any federally funded project — construction, inspection, emergency response.

trigger_2_fhwa.name: FHWA Guidance
trigger_2_fhwa.effect: Federal Highway Administration guidance prohibits DJI/Autel use on any federally funded transportation project. Most Wisconsin DOT work is federally funded — this effectively sidelines DJI on nearly all DOT operations.

trigger_3_fcc.name: FCC Covered List Action
trigger_3_fcc.effect: DJI and foreign-made UAS added to FCC Covered List. Blocks new equipment authorization — no new DJI models can be imported or sold. Existing authorized units can still fly but cannot be replaced.

what_actually_happened.practical_result: Wisconsin state transportation/aviation fleet: 100% grounded for federally funded work. No replacement budget. No compliant alternatives procured. Operations either halted or shifted to state-funded-only projects where legally permissible.

dealer_landscape.dealer_background: Family-owned, established 1945. Authorized DJI dealer. Offices across Midwest including Wisconsin. Authorized dealer for Trimble, ESRI, DJI, and others. Heavy DJI concentration across their commercial and government customer base.
dealer_landscape.dealer_current_position: Still featuring DJI Matrice 400 as flagship product on website as of April 2026. Has not publicly pivoted to NDAA-compliant alternatives.
dealer_landscape.implication: Wisconsin agencies that bought DJI through Seiler now hold grounded assets with no replacement pipeline from their incumbent dealer. Seiler's DJI-first positioning leaves a void that a neutral intelligence platform can fill.

seiler_parent.name: Seiler Instrument & Mfg Company Inc
seiler_parent.note: Seiler's DJI exposure extends across all 9 Midwest states — Wisconsin is one node in a larger dealer-driven DJI concentration problem across the region.

known_active_programs.notes: Launched DFR-adjacent drone program 2022 with 13 FAA-certified pilots. Dramatically improved rural response capabilities. Likely DJI-heavy based on timing and regional dealer landscape.
known_active_programs.status: Fleet compliance status unknown — high probability of DJI exposure

known_active_programs.notes: Used thermal drones to locate fleeing suspect in under 1 minute (October 2024). Active operational drone program.
known_active_programs.status: Platform unknown — likely DJI or Autel based on acquisition timing

known_active_programs.notes: Located missing woman using donated drone technology (March 2025).
Directly cited in NASAO white paper. 100% fleet impacted. 1 airframe reported (state aviation/DOT specific). No replacement budget.

state_policy_context.state_ban_notes: Wisconsin has NOT passed a state-level Chinese drone prohibition (unlike Florida, Arkansas, Tennessee, Nevada). The grounding is federally driven via ASDA/OMB/FHWA/FCC.
state_policy_context.le_warrant_requirement: Wisconsin Statute 175.55 requires law enforcement to obtain a search warrant before using a drone to gather evidence (with exceptions for terrorism, imminent harm to life). Separate from NDAA compliance but adds governance complexity to any DFR program.
state_policy_context.privacy_law: Wisconsin Statute 942.10 — Class A misdemeanor to use drone to observe individual with reasonable expectation of privacy. Does not apply to LE authorized under 175.55.
state_policy_context.counter_drone_bill: Bill circulated Dec 2025 (Rep. Chuck Wichgers / Sen. Julian Bradley) that would authorize police to intercept/disable drones. As of search date, bill not yet passed — legal conflict with 18 USC §32 federal aviation law.
state_policy_context.no_state_replacement_funding: Wisconsin has no dedicated state budget for drone fleet replacement, per NASAO white paper.

total_addressable_market.state_agencies: Wisconsin DOT confirmed. Other state agencies (DNR, emergency management, DOT districts) likely exposed.
total_addressable_market.county_agencies: 72 counties in Wisconsin. Many county sheriff offices, highway departments, and emergency management offices acquired DJI fleets 2018-2024.
total_addressable_market.municipal_agencies: Major cities: Milwaukee, Madison, Green Bay, Kenosha, Racine, Appleton, Waukesha, Oshkosh, Eau Claire, Janesville. Many PDs and FDs acquired DJI.
total_addressable_market.commercial_operators: Survey firms, engineering consultants, construction inspectors across Wisconsin — all working on federally funded projects — similarly grounded for those specific projects.
total_addressable_market.dealer_customer_base: Seiler GeoDrones + other regional DJI dealers' entire Wisconsin customer base is now in transition. This is the widest possible net.

replacement_cost_benchmark.source: Oregon/NASAO white paper + Idaho data point
replacement_cost_benchmark.idaho_example: A $15,000 drone now costs $42,000 to replace with a compliant alternative — 2.8x premium
replacement_cost_benchmark.wisconsin_state_only_exposure: ~$12,000 reported (1 airframe, state DOT) — massively understates true exposure
replacement_cost_benchmark.estimated_true_statewide_exposure: $500K to $5M+ including all local agencies, LE, and commercial operators

urgency_drivers: ASDA phase-out deadline: late 2027 — existing DJI fleets must be replaced on federally funded programs by then
No new DJI replacement parts path — fleet deteriorates without recourse
Grant funding for replacement requires NDAA-compliant platforms — agencies need to know what to buy NOW to qualify
Wisconsin has no state replacement budget — federal grants (COPS, HSGP) are the only realistic funding path
Seiler has not pivoted — there is no trusted advisor telling Wisconsin agencies what to do

forge_opportunity.primary_report: DJI Transition + Grant Eligibility Report — Wisconsin Edition
forge_opportunity.hook: Wisconsin: 100% of state fleet grounded. Is your agency next? Here's what you can buy, what grants will pay for it, and how to start.
free_tier_content: Wisconsin cited as 100% grounded in NASAO white paper (with source link)
ASDA timeline — what deadlines apply to Wisconsin agencies
Grant programs available to Wisconsin agencies (COPS Tech, HSGP/SHSP via Wisconsin Emergency Management, BRIC)
paid_report_content: Full grant eligibility matrix for Wisconsin agencies
Platform recommendation by mission type (patrol, SAR, survey/mapping, construction inspection)
Wisconsin Emergency Management SAA contact and HSGP application cycle
DJI transition timeline and parallel fleet strategy
Seiler alternatives — where Wisconsin agencies can procure NDAA-compliant platforms
Budget estimate for compliant fleet replacement by agency size
target_contacts: Wisconsin Emergency Management (SAA for HSGP grants)
Wisconsin Counties Association (reaches all 72 county agencies at once)

nasao_white_paper_summary.title: Impacts of Federal Restrictions on Foreign-Made Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (UAS) on State Transportation and Aviation Agencies
nasao_white_paper_summary.author: Oregon Department of Aviation
nasao_white_paper_summary.coordinating_body: NASAO (National Association of State Aviation Officials)

key_findings.waiver_recommendation: Waiver until September 2027 to allow realistic transition timeline
key_findings.budget_status: No dedicated replacement budget: OR, GA, IN, MN, NE, ID, NY, WI

policy_recommendations_from_paper: Clear uniform federal guidance on operational eligibility
Transitional allowances for legacy systems (waiver to September 2027)
Federal funding mechanisms to offset replacement costs
Capability-based evaluations rather than origin-based exclusions alone
Improved coordination between OMB, FHWA, FCC, and FAA

miner_config.notes: Check for Revision 3+ — white paper author indicated ongoing data collection. Congressional and FHWA response may trigger update.

midwest_seiler_regional_play.description: Seiler's DJI dealer footprint covers 9 Midwest states. All are exposed to the same fleet grounding problem. Wisconsin is the most acute (100%) but Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Michigan, Missouri, Nebraska are all in the moderate-to-severe impact band per NASAO data.
midwest_seiler_regional_play.forge_play: A Midwest DJI Transition Report covering all 9 Seiler states would be the highest-value commercial report product — specifically targeting the void left by Seiler's failure to pivot. Position as the neutral advisor Seiler should have been.
